summary: The main claim is: While I think anything like this would be much more messy in more useful AIs, it should be possible to extract some useful information from the "short-term goals" of the model.
This post is a transcript of a podcast episode, so it doesn't argue a single main claim — open the original to read the conversation.
The main claim is: I see a lot of posts regarding regulation, usually these involve some sort of phrase like 'Slow Down' or 'Shut Down'.
Is there an actual draft of a proposed bill somewhere that anyone who had the ear of a Senator could point to and say "do that"?
The main claim is: Introduction ------------ The modern story of tech regulation in the United States is a story about China.
Trust me, China is not busy stopping things because of regulation.

The main claim is: Another argument against could be that epsilon is sufficiently small, and therefore n sufficiently high, such that running n systems simultaneously and attempting to daisy chain them would be impossible resource-wise, so will never actuall…
The main claim is: Of particular interest is the ability of these models to reason about novel problems zero-shot, without any direct training on those problems.
In human cognition, this capacity is closely tied to an ability to reason by analogy.

top_comment.author: Roman Leventov
top_comment.text: Also, in the Q&A session of the lecture, people discuss some difficult analogical reasoning tasks that most people resort to solving "symbolically" and iteratively, for example, by trying to apply different possible patterns and mentally check whether there are no logical contradictions, GPT-3 somehow manages to solve too, i.e., in a single auto-regressive rollout. This reminds me of GPT can write Quines now (GPT-4): both these capabilities seem to point to a powerful reasoning capability that Transformers have but people don't.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: This reminds me of GPT can write Quines now (GPT-4): both these capabilities seem to point to a powerful reasoning capability that Transformers have but people don't.

summary: The main claim is: But seriously, AI alignment is an important problem that we need to solve.
Next I insert Elon Musk into the mix,; then I drop alignment and Groucho Marx to Musk.
The main claim is: Actions and observations go from agent to environment and vice-versa.
Environmental states are on the environment side, and internal states are on the agent side.
The main claim is: The top-rated comment on "AGI Ruin: A List of Lethalities" claims that many other people could've written a list like that.
Because I don't actually think it does any good, or persuades anyone of anything, people don't like tests like that, and I don't really believe in them myself either.
